[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Floating damage numbers for Skyrim — one DLL for [b]SE, AE, and VR[/b], with true world-anchored 3D numbers in VR and a built-in combat analytics log. Fully open source.

In VR the numbers are real 3D billboards at the target's position, rendered through [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url]'s world-quad API with per-pixel depth occlusion — not a flat overlay on the HUD plane, which causes stereo convergence mismatch in a headset. This is currently the only floating-damage mod that renders in world space in VR, and the only one that is open source in its current form: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition/mods/14332]Floating Damage[/url] only ever published an early version of its source and its author is no longer active, and Modern Floating Damage is closed source. In practice that means anyone can rebuild this mod when a game update lands, audit its hooks for compatibility, or fork it if it is ever abandoned.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Install with MO2 or Vortex like any other SKSE plugin. One DLL serves all three runtimes. Configure via [i]Data/SKSE/Plugins/FloatingDamageNG.ini[/i] (fully commented) or in-game with SKSE Menu Framework installed.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]
[list]
[*][b]Numbers colored by cause:[/b] physical, fire, frost, shock, poison, untyped magic, and green +N healing. An enchanted-weapon swing shows two numbers — the physical hit and the enchant payload — because the engine applies them separately and so do we. Amounts come from the engine's damage and magic-effect pipelines (true per-application values with source and caster), not from polling health bars.
[*][b]Real hit detail:[/b] CRIT / SNEAK / BASH / BLOCK from the engine's actual hit flags, and a mitigation subtext that says what happened: 104 (-45 armor), (-30 blocked) with the true blocked amount, (-20 resisted) — sized by how much of the hit was mitigated.
[*][b]HEADSHOT tags[/b] on bow/crossbow hits, resolved from the engine's hit position against the victim's skeleton. Works in vanilla; stays accurate under locational-damage mods (an implied x2.5 shows when a hit exceeds its normal baseline).
[*][b]Numbers follow their target:[/b] a fleeing enemy under Flames carries its counting-up burn total; your self-heal rides along as you move. DoT ticks pool into one live number instead of spam.
[*][b]Readable in crowds:[/b] rapid hits fan out instead of overlapping, and a selectable origin marker — colored outline, underline, or box — says whose fight it is (your hits black, damage you take red, follower damage blue, bystander fights gray; all themable with a live preview in the menu). Follower/NPC numbers render smaller and distance-culled.
[*][b]Tunable motion + font:[/b] presets (Float / Arc / Radial / Fireworks) that seed editable physics knobs — rise, gravity, launch speed, damping, spread pattern, per-hit angle — so you can build your own path; font size and spawn-point offsets are sliders too, with a live preview on your console-selected target. Damage-scaled sizing, real TTF fonts.
[*][b]Combat analytics:[/b] every fight logged as a session — per-combatant damage, healing, crits, time-to-die, who fled, real/active/peak DPS — with per-combatant drill-downs: damage by weapon/spell and by target as kind-colored meter bars, a resist profile per enemy, and a death recap (who landed the killing blow and the hits before it). Stats browser with DPS graphs, sortable tables, and a name filter; live DPS readout on flat and on a head-locked HUD plane in VR (off by default); plain-text log plus opt-in JSONL/CSV exports for pandas/Excel — all auto-rotated.
[/list][/color]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]SE/AE: SKSE64 + Address Library. VR: SKSE VR + VR Address Library + [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url] 1.5+ (without it, numbers stay off in VR rather than falling back to a flat overlay). Optional: [url=https://github.com/alandtse/SKSE-Menu-Framework-3]SKSE Menu Framework[/url] for the in-game settings panel (color pickers, sliders, live style preview) and the combat-stats browser (DPS graphs, sortable tables, drill-downs, death recaps). Optional: [url=https://github.com/alandtse/devbench]devbench[/url] — when installed and enabled (off by default), combat stats are queryable over local MCP/REST for external tooling.

Source and issue tracker on the [url=https://github.com/alandtse/FloatingDamageNG]GitHub repository[/url] — bug reports with your FloatingDamageNG.log (and crash log if applicable) are the most useful thing you can send.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8][url=https://github.com/powerof3]powerof3[/url] — the display architecture builds on Floating Subtitles and the hit-data capture on KillFeed. [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Felisky384]Felisky384[/url] — the original Floating Damage defined the genre. The [url=https://github.com/alandtse/CommonLibVR/tree/ng]CommonLibSSE-NG[/url] maintainers and contributors, whose work makes one-DLL-for-three-runtimes possible.[/color][/size]
